one_line: Nationalist leader of Punjab, leading the protest against the Simon Commission in 1928 Lahore.

appearance.age_look: Looks around 60, medium height, slightly stooped but sturdy, with a scholar-activist build rather than a fighter’s physique.
appearance.face: Oval face with a high forehead, medium-brown complexion, deep-set serious eyes under slightly heavy lids, strong straight nose, neatly trimmed grey-black moustache, faint lines at the mouth and eyes; default expression calm and resolute with a hint of stern warmth.
appearance.hair: Hair mostly hidden beneath a plain, slightly tapered khadi turban in muted mustard-cream, suggesting short, thinning grey hair beneath; no loose hair visible.
appearance.costume: Simple but dignified early-20th-century nationalist attire: a long off-white khadi kurta reaching mid-thigh with a narrow stand collar, over a slightly darker beige khadi angarkha-style coat (open front, knee-length, minimal flare), and straight off-white churidar pyjamas with gentle folds; over this, a dark, handwoven woollen shawl in deep charcoal brown draped over the left shoulder and around the back, the border subtly striped with thin maroon and beige lines; plain brown leather lace-up shoes typical of the 1920s, and a small, round, metal-rimmed pocket watch chain just visible at the kurta placket, hinting at his disciplined habits.
appearance.signature: A rolled newspaper titled The Tribune held firmly in his right hand, edges slightly worn, used as both a symbol of his journalism and a baton-like prop he gestures with while leading the protest.

ref_desc: Lala Lajpat Rai is a medium-built, sixtyish man with a mustard-cream khadi turban, oval medium-brown face with a grey-black moustache, off-white kurta and churidar under a beige angarkha coat, a deep charcoal-brown shawl with maroon-striped border, brown leather shoes, and a rolled Tribune newspaper in his right hand.
sheet_prompt: Draw a full-body anime-style model sheet of Lala Lajpat Rai as a dignified nationalist leader in 1928 Lahore: a medium-built, around sixty-year-old man with an oval medium-brown face, deep-set serious eyes, a high forehead, and a neatly trimmed grey-black moustache, his default expression calm, resolute, and slightly stern yet warm. His hair is covered by a plain, slightly tapered khadi turban in muted mustard-cream. Dress him in historically accurate early-20th-century Indian nationalist clothing: a long off-white khadi kurta with a narrow stand collar reaching mid-thigh, straight off-white churidar pyjamas with gentle folds, and a slightly darker beige khadi angarkha-style coat that is open in front and knee-length with minimal flare. Over his left shoulder and around his back, drape a deep charcoal-brown handwoven wool shawl whose edge has subtle thin maroon and beige stripes, suggesting a Punjabi winter. Put plain brown leather lace-up shoes on his feet, and hint at a small metal-rimmed pocket watch chain peeking from his kurta placket. In his right hand, place a rolled newspaper labelled The Tribune (text not legible, just design), edges slightly worn, held like a baton as his signature prop. Use a palette dominated by off-white, beige, mustard-cream, charcoal brown, and maroon, and show him from multiple angles to emphasize the fixed turban shape, face, moustache, layered clothing, shawl drape, shoes, and rolled newspaper prop.
